source_gap_diagnosis.observed_issue: Run 2.56 repairs role-renderer sameness, but the slides still do not explain the product mechanism deeply enough.
source_gap_diagnosis.content_problem: The deck has visual roles and more copy, yet it underspecifies what users provide, what Vulca learns, what code is generated, how comparisons prove value, and what output ships.
source_gap_diagnosis.repair_policy: Add product capability memory before the next generated run. Do not solve this by adding more generic text to the renderer.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca starts from a real commercial brief plus a selected multimodal tutorial, video, transcript, image-reference, and case database instead of a blank prompt.
what_user_provides: constraints for source usage and release
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Show the brief, selected source pack, and source-boundary badge before making any visual-quality claim.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: The next generated run must bind usecase id, source-pack id, and source-boundary status before writing native PPT objects.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render an input-to-output product strip, not a decorative intro slide.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: The audience needs to see that the product is controlled by real business and design inputs, not by vague prompt creativity.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca turns selected sources into design memory, message contracts, and workflow gates that decide what each slide must say and prove.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Show how a real brief becomes structured memory and per-slide obligations.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: Every slide role must reference at least one capability id and one product logic relation id.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render a visible transformation diagram from source evidence to design memory to slide contract.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: This is the product's learning claim; without it, the deck looks like a normal GPT-produced presentation.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca generates editable native PPT objects with code, including layouts, text containers, product surfaces, trace fields, and QA metadata.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Say explicitly that the primary generation mode is code-generated editable PPT, not a raster image deck.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: The next generated run must reject any slide that implies the final deck is image-only.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render editable PPT surfaces as inspectable modules with labels for code module, object type, and trace status.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: The product value is not image generation; it is controllable, inspectable, editable slide generation.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca proves improvement through four arms: prompt-only, Run 1.5 skill baseline, full product-content workflow, and a bad control that removes the new content layer.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Show the audience what the product beats and what it intentionally does not claim to beat yet.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: The next generated run must include competitor-boundary ids and a bad control that can see Run 2.56 but cannot use Run 2.57 content contracts.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render the baseline difference as a product proof, not as a hidden trace-only claim.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: A demo without a comparison can look nice but cannot prove product value.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca records trace manifests, layout checks, source boundaries, content gates, and human review boundaries before publication.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Explain the QA gate as part of the product, not as an internal engineering footnote.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: Every slide must pass reader-question and content-specificity gates before the deck can claim product clarity.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render a compact gate board showing trace, content, layout, source boundary, and human review states.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: The product needs trust. The deck must show why generated output can be reviewed and repaired.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca outputs a native editable PPT deck, previews, trace files, contact sheets, and a viewer that lets the team inspect product proof before release.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Make the final output path concrete: editable PPTX plus viewer and QA artifacts.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: The next generated run must distinguish static editable PPT output from future motion or video proof.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render the handoff as a product surface with deck, viewer, trace, and review artifacts.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: The audience needs to understand what they can try and what is still blocked.

product_capability_records.plain_language_claim: Vulca does not jump to a new stage when quality is weak; it repeats the same case-data-memory-workflow-generation loop and turns defects into stricter contracts.
product_capability_records.visible_ppt_obligation: Show the repair loop as the product operating model, including why Run 2.57 is content-only and Run 2.58 is the next generated proof.
product_capability_records.workflow_contract: If the content is generic, the next run must fail before renderer execution.
product_capability_records.next_renderer_obligation: Render the defect-to-contract-to-rerun loop as the closing product promise.
product_capability_records.why_it_matters: This answers why multiple versions exist and why later versions are not automatically better unless the data/workflow layer improves.

product_logic_relation_records.must_be_visible_as: brief plus source pack becomes capability graph and per-slide message contracts
slide contract selects native PPT renderer obligations before code writes objects
native PPT primitives become editable deck, thumbnails, trace, and viewer output
generated slide objects are checked for content specificity, layout, trace, and source boundary
four-arm comparison is only credited when full arm passes content gates and bad control fails
failed or weak slide review becomes new data/workflow contract before the next generated run

competitor_boundary_records.competitor_pattern: A model writes a deck directly from a prompt without source-bound memory or bad-control proof.
competitor_boundary_records.vulca_difference: Vulca binds a real brief, selected source pack, workflow gates, and code-generated editable PPT before claiming improvement.
competitor_boundary_records.slide_obligation: Show why prompt-only can sound fluent while missing product logic, trace proof, and repeatable repair.

competitor_boundary_records.competitor_pattern: The output is mainly raster imagery or visual mockups.
competitor_boundary_records.vulca_difference: Vulca may use generated images as backgrounds or illustrations, but the main product output is code-generated editable PPT.
competitor_boundary_records.slide_obligation: Say that backgrounds and illustrations are optional assets, while text, layout, trace, and deck structure stay editable.

competitor_boundary_records.competitor_pattern: A user fills a template or theme with generic content blocks.
competitor_boundary_records.vulca_difference: Vulca derives slide obligations from usecase data, product capability memory, and workflow gates, then generates code-generated editable PPT.
competitor_boundary_records.slide_obligation: Contrast template reuse against evidence-bound per-slide message contracts.

competitor_boundary_records.competitor_pattern: A designer manually arranges visual components in a canvas editor.
competitor_boundary_records.vulca_difference: Vulca creates a code-generated editable PPT artifact with traceable data, workflow, and QA, then leaves room for human design review.
competitor_boundary_records.slide_obligation: Show automation plus inspection, not a claim that human visual judgment is unnecessary.

competitor_boundary_records.competitor_pattern: Office-native assistance can create or rewrite slides, but the generation path may be hard to audit as a case database plus workflow experiment.
competitor_boundary_records.vulca_difference: Vulca makes the case database, skill workflow, bad control, trace manifest, and code-generated editable PPT contract visible.
competitor_boundary_records.slide_obligation: Frame Vulca as an experimentable product loop, not just a helper inside an existing slide editor.
